_comment: Example configuration file for pair-review. Copy to ~/.pair-review/config.json and modify as needed.
_github_token_command_comment: Shell command whose stdout is used as the GitHub token. Runs once and is cached. Default: 'gh auth token'.
_summaries_comment: Configures the inline natural-language hunk-summary feature. enabled gates the feature entirely; auto_generate controls whether a summary job kicks off automatically on review load (false = require a manual click on the toolbar button). provider/model fall back to default_provider/default_model and the provider's fast-tier model. max_files/max_lines_added are perf caps that skip generation for overly large diffs.
_tours_comment: Configures the guided-tour feature. enabled gates the feature entirely; auto_generate controls whether a tour job kicks off automatically on review load (false = require a manual click on the toolbar button). provider/model fall back through summaries.provider, default_provider, and the provider's fast-tier model.
_external_comments_comment: Opt-in feature toggle for fetching and displaying GitHub PR review comments inline. Default: false. Set to true to enable the External segment, refresh button, and /api/reviews/*/external-comments* routes.

providers._comment: Provider configurations override built-in defaults. Most providers have built-in models, but OpenCode and Pi require manual configuration.

opencode._comment: OpenCode has NO built-in models - you MUST configure models here to use it.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4
models.description: Fast and capable for most reviews
models.tagline: Best balance of speed and quality

models.name: Claude Opus 4
models.description: Most capable model for complex reviews

models.name: GPT-4.1
models.description: OpenAI's latest model for deep analysis

claude._comment: Override Claude's built-in configuration. Useful for custom paths, wrapper scripts, or additional arguments. Built-in model IDs: fable-5-xhigh, fable-5-high, opus-4.8-xhigh, opus-4.8-high, opus-4.7-xhigh, opus-4.7-high, opus-4.6-high, sonnet-5-xhigh, sonnet-5-high, sonnet-4.6, opus-4.6-1m, and haiku ('opus' and 'fable' are convenience aliases for opus-4.8-xhigh and fable-5-xhigh). Use 'cli_model' to decouple the app-level model ID from the CLI --model argument. Use 'env' for model-specific environment variables.
claude._comment_default_model: Provider-level 'default_model' selects which model is the default in the picker and when no model is specified. Preferred over the per-model 'default: true' flag (which is deprecated). If it names an unknown or disabled model, the provider falls back to automatic selection.
claude._comment_disabled_models: 'disabled_models' hides specific models (built-in or config-defined) from the picker for this provider. A list that would hide every model is ignored. disabled_models matches by model id or alias, so either a canonical id or a convenience alias works. Example below removes the fast-tier 'haiku' model and the thorough-tier Fable 5 model (named here by its 'fable' alias).

models._comment: Example: simple override — customize the display name and description of a built-in model. Note: 'tier' is required even for overrides (valid values: fast, balanced, thorough, premium).
models.name: Sonnet (Team Default)
models.description: Our team's standard model for reviews

models._comment: Example: override cli_model to use a specific version string for the CLI --model flag
Example: cli_model set to null suppresses --model entirely (model set via env instead)
models.name: Custom Env Model
models.description: Model specified entirely via environment variable

models._comment: Example: adding a custom model with extra arguments and env
models.name: Custom Model
models.description: Example of adding a custom model with extra arguments

cursor-agent._comment: Override Cursor Agent's built-in configuration. Uses streaming JSON output with sandbox mode enabled by default.

pi._comment: Pi has NO built-in models - you MUST configure models here to use it. Below is just an example of that configuration format.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4
models.description: Fast and capable for most reviews
models.tagline: Best balance of speed and quality

models.name: Claude Opus 4
models.description: Most capable model for complex reviews

my-review-tool._comment: Example executable provider. Runs an external CLI tool and maps its output to pair-review's suggestion format using an LLM. The tool's raw output is transformed via mapping_instructions.
my-review-tool.name: My Review Tool
my-review-tool.mapping_instructions: The tool outputs JSON with a 'findings' array and a 'summary' string. Each finding has: category (bug/style/perf), priority (high/medium/low), title, body, fix, path, start_line, end_line. Map path to file, body to description, fix to suggestion. Map priority to severity: high=critical, medium=medium, low=minor. Map confidence: high=0.9, medium=0.7, low=0.4.
my-review-tool._comment_availability: availability_command is run at startup to decide if the provider is available (defaults to 'true', i.e. always available). availability_timeout_seconds bounds that check (default 10); raise it when the command runs a slow build/compile step. Also supported on chat_providers entries.

chat_providers._comment: Chat provider command overrides. Separate from 'providers' (AI analysis). Chat providers are: pi, claude, codex, copilot-acp, gemini-acp, opencode-acp, cursor-acp. Supports 'command', 'args' (replaces defaults), 'extra_args' (appends), 'env', 'load_skills' (Pi: false suppresses skill auto-discovery), and 'app_extensions' (Pi: false omits pair-review task extension) overrides. Codex also supports 'sandbox' ('workspace-write' or 'read-only'). 'availability_command' overrides the startup availability probe and 'availability_timeout_seconds' bounds it (default 10; raise for slow build commands).

claude._comment: Example: use a wrapper script for Claude chat

codex._comment: Example: use a wrapper script for Codex chat. Use sandbox='read-only' for discussion-only sessions.

repos._comment: Repository configurations (renamed from 'monorepos'; the old key still works). 'path' points to an existing local clone. 'checkout_script' runs after worktree creation. 'reset_script' runs when switching a pool worktree to a new PR. 'load_skills' controls whether AI providers load environment-level skills (default: true). 'skip_bulk_fetch' opts out of the unconditional `git fetch <remote> --prune` during refresh (default: false); the targeted base-SHA and PR-head ref fetches still run. Useful for very large monorepos where bulk fetching all refs/tags is prohibitively slow.
